text.title: Five-Step Accessibility Testing Process
text.summary: Sequential testing process from automated scanning through screen reader and cognitive verification, with tool selection and explicit coverage boundaries for each step.

rule.statement: Run both axe AND Pa11y on every audit — their rule sets diverge on ~15% of issues; use the union of violations, not the intersection.
rule.rationale: axe-core and Pa11y have different heuristics and catch different subsets of WCAG violations. A scan with only one tool produces a systematically incomplete picture.

example.code:
What automated tools find: - Missing alt text (axe 1.1.1) - Color contrast failures (axe 1.4.3, 1.4.11) - Missing form labels (axe 1.3.1, 4.1.2) - Incorrect heading structure (axe 1.3.1) - Missing language attribute (axe 3.1.1)
What they miss: - Alt text quality (meaningful vs. generic) - Keyboard operability and trap detection - Reading order vs. visual order - Context and user understanding - WCAG 2.2 drag alternatives and redundant entry

antipattern.description: Treating "0 violations" in axe as equivalent to WCAG conformance.
antipattern.reason: axe covers ~30-40% of WCAG criteria. The "incomplete" and "inapplicable" arrays in axe JSON must be inspected; items in "incomplete" mean the tool gave up, not that the criterion passes.

rule.statement: Tab through the entire page without touching the mouse; every interactive element must be reachable, operable, and have a visible focus indicator.
rule.rationale: Screen reader users navigate by keyboard; axe cannot detect keyboard traps, illogical tab order, or missing skip links — these require a human Tab walkthrough.

example.code: Check | How to Test | Pass Condition Tab navigation | Tab through entire page | All interactive elements reachable Focus visible | Watch for focus indicator | Always visible; contrast >= 3:1 Tab order | Tab and note sequence | Matches visual/logical flow Skip links | Tab from top of page | "Skip to main content" appears Keyboard traps | Tab everywhere | Can always Tab out Custom components | Test accordions, tabs, modals | APG patterns followed

rule.statement: Test with at least NVDA+Firefox and VoiceOver+Safari; a pass on one AT+browser combo does not generalize to another.
rule.rationale: Screen readers and browsers diverge on ARIA interpretation. NVDA+JAWS differ on Windows for the same DOM; mobile AT (VoiceOver iOS, TalkBack) have additional gesture-specific behaviors.

list.item: Navigate by headings (H key) — hierarchy correct and present?
Navigate by landmarks (D key in NVDA) — main, nav, header, footer marked?
All images described — meaningful alt text announced?
Forms: label announced on focus; errors announced on invalid submit?
Dynamic content: live region updates announced (ARIA live)?
Can complete key user tasks end-to-end using only AT?
Clear language: plain language, no jargon, Flesch reading ease >= 60.
Error prevention: confirmation before destructive actions.
Error recovery: clear error messages with actionable solutions; non-blaming tone.
Timeouts: warnings before timeout; option to extend or eliminate.
Animation: can be paused; no excessive motion; respects prefers-reduced-motion.

section.title: Step 5: Document and Fix

rule.statement: Every a11y bug must be linked to a WCAG criterion number and include a pre-fix screenshot; never close a bug without both.
rule.rationale: Without criterion mapping and evidence, audit drift is undetectable and VPAT/ACR cannot be produced from the issue tracker.

section.reference: Full audit report template with executive summary and per-criterion findings table
